mission.title: Install and Configure Docker Desktop Kubernetes
mission.description: Docker Desktop for Mac provides an integrated Docker experience with Kubernetes support for local development. It simplifies container management and orchestration, making it ideal for developers who need a local Kubernetes environment. Key trade-offs include limited scalability compared to cloud solutions and potential resource constraints on local machines.

steps.title: Download Docker Desktop for Mac
steps.description:
This step involves downloading the Docker Desktop application, which includes Kubernetes support.

```bash

open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac

```

> This opens the download page for Docker Desktop.

steps.title: Install Docker Desktop
steps.description:
Run the downloaded installer to set up Docker Desktop on your Mac.

```bash

hdiutil attach ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

cp -r /Volumes/Docker/Docker.app /Applications/

hdiutil detach /Volumes/Docker

```

> Mount the downloaded Docker image.
> Copy Docker to the Applications folder.
> Unmount the Docker image.

steps.title: Enable Kubernetes
steps.description:
Configure Docker Desktop to enable Kubernetes support.

```bash

open -a Docker

sleep 10 && osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click menu item "Preferences…" of menu "Docker" of menu bar 1'

osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click checkbox "Enable Kubernetes"'

osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click button "Apply & Restart"'

```

> Open Docker Desktop application.
> Open preferences.
> Enable Kubernetes in the settings.
> Apply changes and restart Docker.
commands: sleep 10 && osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click menu item "Preferences…" of menu "Docker" of menu bar 1'
osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click checkbox "Enable Kubernetes"'
osascript -e 'tell application "System Events" to tell process "Docker" to click button "Apply & Restart"'

steps.title: Verify Kubernetes Installation
steps.description:
Check if Kubernetes is running correctly within Docker Desktop.

```bash

kubectl version --short

kubectl get nodes

```

> Verify the Kubernetes client and server versions.
> List the nodes in the Kubernetes cluster.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Docker Desktop
uninstall.description:
This will remove Docker Desktop and all associated Kubernetes resources. Be aware that any local containers and images will also be deleted.

```bash

osascript -e 'tell application "Docker" to quit'

brew uninstall --cask docker

# Docker data cleaned up by brew uninstall

```

> Ensure Docker is not running.
> Remove Docker Desktop application.
> Remove Docker's container data.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Docker Desktop
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up your Docker data. This step will involve downloading the latest version and installing it.

```bash

open https://store.docker.com/editions/community/docker-ce-desktop-mac

hdiutil attach ~/Downloads/Docker.dmg

cp -r /Volumes/Docker/Docker.app /Applications/

hdiutil detach /Volumes/Docker

open -a Docker

```

> Download the latest version of Docker Desktop.
> Mount the new Docker image.
> Copy the new version to Applications.
> Unmount the new Docker image.
> Start Docker Desktop to complete the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Kubernetes cluster is not starting
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Insufficient resources allocated to Docker Desktop.

**Fix:**
Increase CPU and memory allocation in Docker Desktop settings.

troubleshooting.title: kubectl command not found
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** kubectl is not installed or not in the PATH.

**Fix:**
Install kubectl using 'brew install kubectl' or ensure it's in your PATH.

troubleshooting.title: Error: Unable to connect to the server: dial tcp 127.0.0.1:6443: connect: connection refused
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Kubernetes is not running or has crashed.

**Fix:**
Restart Docker Desktop and check the Kubernetes settings.

troubleshooting.title: Kubernetes version mismatch
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** Docker Desktop is running an outdated Kubernetes version.

**Fix:**
Update Docker Desktop to the latest version to get the latest Kubernetes version.

**Affected versions:** <= 4.31

versionNotes.changes: Updated Kubernetes to v1.30, improved integration with Docker Compose.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'kubectl get nodes' showing the nodes in the cluster with a status of 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Mac meets the minimum requirements for Docker Desktop.
